$comment: C23 _BitInt(N>64) MULTI-LIMB multiply + divide/remainder (D-CSUBSET-BITINT-C3-MULDIV), C3: schoolbook `*` via the 64x64->128 UMulH primitive, binary long-division `/` `%` (signed + unsigned, bit-precise wrap). Builds on the C2 by-address wide substrate. Every operand is a RUNTIME value (volatile-seeded) so no const-fold pre-evaluates the wide arithmetic. RED-ON-DISABLE: an OR-carry multiply (dropping a column carry-out of 2, CRIT-A) reads limb 3 of the crafted a=[1,M,0,0] * b=[M,1,M,0] product as 2 instead of 3 (an all-ones square does NOT expose this — lo=M*M mod 2^64=1 is too small to fire the second carry); a low-limb-only / non-wrapping multiply breaks the cross-limb + wrap cases; a long-division whose temps use the SIGNED type instead of the mandated unsigned one (CRIT-B) inverts emitWideOrder's top-limb compare when the divisor magnitude sets the i64 sign bit -> the N=128 most-negative-divisor case (mn/mn=1) breaks; the non-64-multiple _BitInt(200) most-negative dividend (CRIT-C) covers the magnitude path where maskTopLimb is not a no-op; a broken C99 sign fixup breaks the four-sign-combo divide/modulo; a single-limb divide breaks the multi-limb quotient+remainder. Each term is boolean-gated -> the sum is 42 only when the multi-limb machinery is correct. Wide arithmetic is materialized by ADDRESS (never a bare SSA aggregate), so the release arm's mem2reg/const-fold preserve the multiply carry chains + the long-division loop and still exit 42. Divide-by-zero is NOT exercised here (it hard-traps like narrow idiv #DE); it has its own unit pin.
